pr.title: Split more of sysdolphin out into respective .s files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-only sysdolphin organization PR that removed the monolithic asm/text_3.s path and split sysdolphin code into named .s files up to the start of the HSD AX wrapper functions. The linker script was updated to list the new object order, code_8037F244.s was narrowed/renamed to code_8038838C.s, code_80379310.s became mtx.s, and references in text_1.s were renamed to known HSD symbols such as HSD_ArchiveParse, HSD_ShadowAlloc, and HSD_ViewingRectAddRect. There were no review comments in the captured PR slice.
what_changed: Removed the previous non-split sysdolphin text_3.o entry from ldscript.lcf and replaced it with named sysdolphin objects, including state.o, tev.o, mtx.o, util.o, shadow.o, archive.o, random.o, bytecode.o, class.o, hash.o, texp.o, texpdag.o, leak.o, debug.o, and code_8038838C.o.

Split a large sysdolphin assembly range out of asm/code_8037F244.s into focused files such as asm/archive.s, asm/bytecode.s, asm/class.s, asm/debug.s, asm/hash.s, asm/leak.s, asm/random.s, asm/shadow.s, asm/state.s, asm/texp.s, asm/texpdag.s, and asm/util.s.
Renamed asm/text_3.s to asm/tev.s and reduced it by moving most of its contents into more specific sysdolphin assembly files.
Renamed asm/code_80379310.s to asm/mtx.s, reflecting a known subsystem/module identity.
Renamed asm/code_8037F244.s to asm/code_8038838C.s after splitting earlier sysdolphin code out of it, leaving the remaining address-based blob beginning later in the text range.

Updated call-site symbol names in asm/text_1.s from address-style func_* names to known HSD names, including func_803801E4 to HSD_ArchiveParse, func_8037F280 to HSD_ShadowAlloc, and func_8038001C to HSD_ViewingRectAddRect.
New split files consistently include macros.inc and emit into the .text section with the repository's assembly address/comment style.
smart_moves: Kept linker order explicit while splitting the monolithic sysdolphin range, which is critical for preserving addresses and matching behavior in an assembly-backed decompilation.
Used subsystem/module file names such as archive.s, bytecode.s, shadow.s, state.s, tev.s, texp.s, and texpdag.s instead of leaving everything under generic text_3.s or code_* blobs.
Preserved address-based fallback naming only for the unsplit remainder, code_8038838C.s, which makes the boundary of completed splitting easy to search and revisit.
Updated existing call sites to use newly identified HSD globals rather than only changing file boundaries, improving cross-file readability without claiming a C decompilation.
Made a mechanical, low-risk reorganization: most changes are assembly relocation and ldscript object order updates rather than logic edits.
decomp_lessons: When a large unknown assembly blob contains multiple recognizable library modules, splitting it by subsystem and updating ldscript.lcf can improve navigability before any C matching work starts.
Object order in ldscript.lcf is part of the match; split files should be inserted exactly where their text ranges occur, not merely grouped alphabetically.
After splitting out known modules, rename only the residual code_* file to its new starting address so future contributors know exactly what remains unsplit.
Call-site renames in older asm files are useful once a global symbol becomes identified; they turn address-style calls into searchable subsystem references.
It is acceptable for early decomp infrastructure PRs to consist mostly of assembly file factoring, provided the labels, globals, and linker script preserve the original layout.
naming_conventions: Known sysdolphin subsystem assembly files use short lowercase module names: archive.s, bytecode.s, class.s, debug.s, hash.s, leak.s, mtx.s, random.s, shadow.s, state.s, tev.s, texp.s, texpdag.s, util.s.
Known HAL/sysdolphin globals use HSD_ prefixes, as shown by HSD_ArchiveParse, HSD_ByteCodeEval, HSD_ShadowAlloc, and HSD_ViewingRectAddRect.
Residual unsplit assembly blobs are named by their starting address, such as code_8038838C.s.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Split monolithic assembly into multiple .s files while keeping each file in .section .text and including macros.inc.
Update ldscript.lcf object list to match the exact split order so relocated assembly still links at the expected addresses.
Use file renames for contiguous residual ranges rather than deleting/recreating everything; examples include code_8037F244.s to code_8038838C.s and text_3.s to tev.s.
Rename branch/call targets in existing assembly when a function's known HSD name is established, as seen in text_1.s call sites.
Use address comments and global labels to preserve traceability from original binary offsets during large mechanical splits.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the captured PR context.
follow_up_queries: Which later PRs decompile the sysdolphin assembly files introduced here into C?
When were HSD_ArchiveParse and HSD_ByteCodeEval moved from assembly to C?
Which PR continued splitting or decompiling code_8038838C.s after this boundary?
Are there subsequent naming corrections for shadow, archive, tev, texp, or texpdag symbols introduced by this split?
